cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user is asking what Steve Rowson thinks about the newer football helmets. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the text. The article mentions Steve Rowson as an engineer at Virginia Tech who tests helmets. It says, "No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So, the key point here is that the newer helmets reduce the speed gain of the head after impact, which in turn lowers concussion risk. The question is asking what Rowson thinks of the newer helmets. Looking at the options: A. They vary a lot in size. The text doesn't mention size variation, so probably not. B. They work better than the old ones. The text says newer helmets make the head not gain speed as much, implying improvement over older ones. So this could be correct. C. They slow down the player's running speed. The article talks about the head's speed after a hit, not the player's running speed. So C is likely incorrect. D.
